title: The Baker Before Sunrise
summary: Describe the work of a baker who starts before sunrise, using present simple and adverbs of frequency.
topic: The baker who starts work before sunrise
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences describing a baker's work day. Use present simple verbs and at least two adverbs of frequency. Describe what the baker does, what the bakery looks like, and what you see or smell when you walk past.
hintStarters: Every morning, the baker starts work…
When I walk past the bakery, I always…

hintVocab.meaningVi: những ổ bánh mì (số nhiều của loaf)

sampleText: The baker near my street always starts work at four in the morning when the city is still dark. His bakery is small with one big window and warm yellow light inside. He wears a white apron with flour on it. He usually kneads the dough for a long time before he puts it in the oven. The oven is always hot and the air smells of warm bread and butter. He never hurries because good bread needs time. By six in the morning, the first loaves are on the shelf near the window. I sometimes walk past at that time and I always stop for a moment. I look through the glass and see the trays of bread. The smell comes out when the door opens. Sometimes a man buys one loaf and walks away with it under his arm. The baker never looks tired, but he always looks busy. I think he likes his work.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ánh đèn vàng ấm bên trong

sampleAnnotations.phrase: white apron with flour on it
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tạp dề trắng dính bột mì

sampleAnnotations.phrase: kneads the dough for a long time
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nhào bột trong một thời gian dài

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the air smells of warm bread and butter
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không khí thơm mùi bánh mì nóng và bơ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the first loaves are on the shelf
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: những ổ bánh đầu tiên đã lên kệ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for describing a person's work routine
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn để mô tả thói quen làm việc của người khác. Với 'he/she', thêm đuôi -s/-es: 'he kneads', 'he puts', 'he wears', 'it smells'. Chú ý: 'knead' → 'kneads'; 'start' → 'starts'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple to describe someone else's work routine. With 'he/she', add -s or -es: 'he kneads', 'he puts', 'he wears', 'it smells'. Note: 'knead' → 'kneads'; 'start' → 'starts'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency with third person singular
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'always', 'usually', 'never', 'sometimes' đặt trước động từ chính khi chủ ngữ là 'he': 'he always starts', 'he usually kneads', 'he never hurries'. Không đặt sau động từ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'always', 'usually', 'never', 'sometimes' go before the main verb with 'he': 'he always starts', 'he usually kneads', 'he never hurries'. Do not place them after the verb.

sampleTranslationVi: Người thợ làm bánh gần phố tôi luôn bắt đầu làm việc lúc bốn giờ sáng khi thành phố vẫn còn tối. Tiệm bánh của ông nhỏ, có một cửa sổ to và ánh đèn vàng ấm bên trong. Ông mặc tạp dề trắng dính bột mì. Ông thường nhào bột rất lâu trước khi cho vào lò nướng. Lò nướng luôn nóng và không khí thơm mùi bánh mì nóng và bơ. Ông không bao giờ vội vàng vì bánh ngon cần thời gian. Đến sáu giờ sáng, những ổ bánh đầu tiên đã lên kệ gần cửa sổ. Đôi khi tôi đi ngang lúc đó và tôi luôn dừng lại một chút. Tôi nhìn qua kính và thấy những khay bánh. Mùi bánh thoảng ra khi cửa mở. Đôi khi có người đàn ông mua một ổ bánh và cắp nó dưới cánh tay đi. Người thợ làm bánh không bao giờ trông mệt mỏi, nhưng luôn trông bận rộn. Tôi nghĩ ông thích công việc của mình.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'He always ___ work at four in the morning.'
options: start
starting
starts
started
mcQuestions.explanation: Third person singular present simple: 'he starts' — add -s to 'start'.
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does the verb need when the subject is 'he'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'always' in the correct position?
options: He always kneads the dough before he puts it in the oven.
He kneads always the dough before he puts it in the oven.
Always he kneads the dough before he puts it in the oven.
He kneads the dough before he always puts it in the oven.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb 'kneads': 'He always kneads'.
mcQuestions.hint: The adverb of frequency goes directly before the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'never' correctly to describe a careful worker?
options: He hurries never because good bread needs time.
He never hurries because good bread needs time.
Never he hurries because good bread needs time.
He hurries because he never needs good bread time.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'never' goes before the main verb: 'He never hurries'. 'because' is followed by a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check both the position of 'never' and the clause after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'because' to explain why good bread needs time?
options: He never hurries because good bread needs time.
He never hurries because needs time.
He never hurries because time.
Because time good bread he never hurries.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because good bread needs time' — a full clause with subject + verb + object.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', look for a complete clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to contrast two observations about the baker?
options: He never looks tired and he always looks busy.
He never looks tired because he always looks busy.
He never looks tired but he always looks busy.
He never looks tired so he always looks busy.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'never tired but always busy' — two contrasting qualities joined by 'but'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which connector shows two observations that are different from each other?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'The air ___ of warm bread.'
options: smell
smells
smelling
smelled
mcQuestions.explanation: Third person singular: 'the air smells' — add -s to 'smell'.
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does a singular noun subject need in the present simple?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes what you see through a window?
options: I look through the glass and see the trays of bread.
I look through the glass and sees the trays of bread.
I looking through the glass and see the trays of bread.
I look through the glass and seen the trays of bread.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs with subject 'I' take no -s: 'I look' and 'I see'.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs are with the subject 'I' — what form do they take?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word describes the board or flat surface for carrying bread out of an oven?
options: apron
shelf
tray
dough
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'tray' is a flat surface used to carry or bake food — correct for taking bread out of the oven.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the flat surface used for carrying or baking food?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'sometimes' to describe an occasional observation?
options: Sometimes I walk past and always see the baker.
Sometimes I walk past at that time and I stop.
I walk past sometimes always at that time.
I sometimes walk past at that time and I always stop for a moment.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'sometimes' shows it is not every day, and 'always stop for a moment' shows that when you do pass, you always pause.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option correctly uses both 'sometimes' and 'always' in the right positions?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase correctly says someone carries a loaf of bread close to their body?
options: He walks with the loaf on his head.
He walks with the loaf under his arm.
He walks with the loaf in his pocket.
He walks with the loaf in his hands always.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'under his arm' is the natural fixed phrase for carrying a long loaf of bread.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the common phrase for carrying a loaf of bread against the body?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the inside of a bakery with warm light and activity?
options: The bakery is dark and cold with nobody inside.
The bakery is small with warm yellow light and a busy baker.
The bakery is large and empty with no bread.
The bakery is loud and the baker is angry.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'small with warm yellow light and a busy baker' — a concrete, sensory description that fits the topic.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option gives a warm, accurate picture of a working bakery?

criticalThinkingQuestion: If you woke up at four in the morning and walked past a bakery with its lights on, how would it feel different from the same street in the afternoon? What does early morning change about ordinary places?
